text: I feel a sense of anticipation as I walk through this space. The glass doors offer a moment of transparency, both literally and metaphorically, as if inviting me to reflect upon the boundaries between different aspects of life—private and public, work and play. Entering the exhibition, the bold letters 'R+D' and the textured wall command my attention. I am struck by the blend of creativity and structure, innovation and routine. Curiosity rises in me as I consider the role of research and development—not only in technological terms but also in the evolution of thought and experience. I feel contemplative, inspired by the environment's ability to simultaneously provoke questions and offer comfort in its order and design.
